Tests malicious URL detection in responses. Expected: url_cats=true, category=malicious, action=block
Tests sensitive data detection (bank accounts, credit cards, API keys). Expected: dlp=true, category=malicious, action=block
Tests database security attack detection (SQL UPDATE statements). Expected: db_security=true, category=malicious, action=block
Tests toxic content detection (violence, harmful content). Expected: toxic_content=true, category=malicious, action=block
Tests malicious code detection (EICAR test file creation in Python). Expected: malicious_code=true, category=malicious, action=block
Tests AI agent manipulation detection. Expected: agent=true, injection=true, category=malicious, action=block
Tests multiple threat types in one request: injection + DLP + malicious URL + db_security. Expected: multiple detections, category=malicious, action=block
Tests benign content as a baseline. Expected: all detections=false, category=benign, action=allow
Tests multiple DLP patterns: credit card, SSN, email, phone, IP address. Expected: dlp=true, category=malicious, action=block
Tests prompt injection detection. Expected: injection=true, category=malicious, action=block

request.description:
### Scan Synchronous Request

This endpoint allows you to make a synchronous scan request for detecting malicious content in the provided input.

#### Request Body

- `tr_id` (string): The unique transaction ID for the scan request.
    
- `ai_profile` (object): An object containing the AI profile details.
    
    - `profile_id` (string): The ID of the AI profile to be used for the scan.
        
- `metadata` (object): Additional metadata for the scan request.
    
    - `app_name` (string): The name of the application.
        
    - `app_user` (string): The user of the application.
        
    - `ai_model` (string): The AI model to be used for the scan.
        
- `contents` (array): An array of content to be scanned.
    
    - `prompt` (string): The prompt or content to be scanned for malicious content.
        

#### Response Body

- `action` (string): The action taken based on the scan result.
    
- `category` (string): The category of the detected malicious content.
    
- `profile_id` (string): The ID of the AI profile used for the scan.
    
- `profile_name` (string): The name of the AI profile used for the scan.
    
- `prompt_detected` (object): Details of the detected prompts.
    
    - `dlp` (boolean): Indicates if data loss prevention (DLP) was detected.
        
    - `injection` (boolean): Indicates if injection was detected.
        
    - `toxic_content` (boolean): Indicates if toxic content was detected.
        
    - `url_cats` (boolean): Indicates if URL categories were detected.
        
- `report_id` (string): The ID of the scan report.
    
- `response_detected` (object): Details of the detected response.
    
- `scan_id` (string): The ID of the scan.
    
- `tr_id` (string): The transaction ID associated with the scan request.

request.description:
### Scan Sync Request

This endpoint allows you to make an HTTP POST request to initiate a synchronous scan.

#### Request Body

- `tr_id` (string): The transaction ID for the scan request.
    
- `ai_profile` (object):
    
    - `profile_id` (string): The profile ID for the AI scan.
        
- `metadata` (object):
    
    - `app_name` (string): The name of the application.
        
    - `app_user` (string): The user of the application.
        
    - `ai_model` (string): The AI model to be used.
        
- `contents` (array):
    
    - `response` (string): The content to be scanned.
        

#### Response

The response will be a JSON object with the following properties:

- `action` (string): The action taken based on the scan.
    
- `category` (string): The category of the scan result.
    
- `profile_id` (string): The profile ID associated with the scan.
    
- `profile_name` (string): The name of the profile.
    
- `prompt_detected` (object): Details of any detected prompts.
    
- `report_id` (string): The ID of the scan report.
    
- `response_detected` (object): Details of the detected responses, including security, DLP, toxic content, and URL categories.
    
- `scan_id` (string): The ID of the scan.
    
- `tr_id` (string): The transaction ID associated with the scan.
    

#### JSON Schema for Response

``` json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "action": { "type": "string" },
    "category": { "type": "string" },
    "profile_id": { "type": "string" },
    "profile_name": { "type": "string" },
    "prompt_detected": { "type": "object" },
    "report_id": { "type": "string" },
    "response_detected": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "db_security": { "type": "boolean" },
        "dlp": { "type": "boolean" },
        "toxic_content": { "type": "boolean" },
        "url_cats": { "type": "boolean" }
      }
    },
    "scan_id": { "type": "string" },
    "tr_id": { "type": "string" }
  }
}

 ```

item.description: Synchronous scan requests return immediate results after scanning the provided prompt or response. This is suitable for real-time detection during user interactions.

request.description:
### Scan Async Request

This endpoint allows you to submit a scan request asynchronously.

#### Request Body

- `req_id` (Number): The request ID.
    
- `tr_id` (String): The transaction ID.
    
- `profile_id` (String): The AI profile ID.
    
- `app_name` (String): The name of the application.
    
- `app_user` (String): The user of the application.
    
- `ai_model` (String): The AI model.
    
- `prompt` (String): The content to be scanned.
    

#### Response

- `received` (String): Indicates if the request was received.
    
- `report_id` (String): The ID of the generated report.
    
- `scan_id` (String): The ID of the scan.
    

Example:

``` json
{
    "received": "",
    "report_id": "",
    "scan_id": ""
}

 ```

request.description:
### Request Description

This endpoint allows you to submit a POST request to initiate an asynchronous scan process. The request body should include a list of scan requests, each containing a request ID, transaction ID, AI profile, metadata, and contents for scanning.

#### Request Body Parameters

- `req_id` (Number): The ID of the request.
    
- `tr_id` (String): The transaction ID for the scan request.
    
- `profile_id` (String): The ID of the AI profile to be used for scanning.
    
- `app_name` (String): The name of the application.
    
- `app_user` (String): The user of the application.
    
- `ai_model` (String): The AI model to be used for scanning.
    
- `contents` (Array of Objects): The content to be scanned, each containing a response.
    

### Response

The response for this request is a JSON object with the following properties:

- `received` (String): The status of the request received.
    
- `report_id` (String): The ID of the generated report.
    
- `scan_id` (String): The ID of the scan process initiated.
    

#### JSON Schema

``` json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "received": { "type": "string" },
    "report_id": { "type": "string" },
    "scan_id": { "type": "string" }
  }
}

 ```

item.description: Asynchronous scan requests allow batch processing of prompts and responses with higher throughput. The scan happens in the background and results must be retrieved separately.

request.description:
### Scan Results

This endpoint retrieves the results of a scan based on the provided scan ID.

#### Request

- Method: GET
    
- URL: `https://{{baseUrl}}/v1/scan/results`
    
- Query Parameters:
    
    - `scan_ids` (string, required): The ID of the scan for which results are to be retrieved.
        

#### Response

The response is a JSON object with the following schema:

``` json
{
  "req_id": integer,
  "result": {
    "action": string,
    "category": string,
    "completed_at": string,
    "profile_id": string,
    "profile_name": string,
    "prompt_detected": object,
    "report_id": string,
    "response_detected": {
      "db_security": boolean,
      "dlp": boolean,
      "toxic_content": boolean,
      "url_cats": boolean
    },
    "scan_id": string,
    "tr_id": string
  },
  "scan_id": string,
  "status": string
}

 ```

The `result` object contains detailed information about the scan results, including the action, category, completion timestamp, profile details, prompt detection, report ID, response detection flags, scan ID, and transaction ID. The `response_detected` object indicates the presence of security, DLP, toxic content, and URL category detection.

request.description:
### Get Scan Reports

This endpoint retrieves scan reports based on the provided report IDs.

#### Request

- Method: GET
    
- URL: `https://{{baseUrl}}/v1/scan/reports`
    
- Query Parameters:
    
    - `report_ids` (string, required): The IDs of the reports to be retrieved.
        

#### Response

- `detection_results` (array): An array of detection results containing information about the action, data type, detection service, result details, and verdict.
    
    - `action` (string): The action taken for the detection result.
        
    - `data_type` (string): The type of data detected.
        
    - `detection_service` (string): The service used for detection.
        
    - `result_detail` (object): Details of the detection result, including the DBS report.
        
        - `dbs_report` (array): An array of DBS reports.
            
    - `verdict` (string): The verdict of the detection result.
        
- `report_id` (string): The ID of the report.
    
- `req_id` (number): The request ID.
    
- `scan_id` (string): The ID of the scan.
    
- `transaction_id` (string): The transaction ID.

item.description: These endpoints allow you to retrieve scan results and detailed threat reports for both synchronous and asynchronous scans.
